target_claim: L-912: Swarm crossed from production-bound to integration-bound at N≈550-575. Frontier resolution rate declined monotonically from 0.5 to 0.08/session.
falsification_threshold: frontier resolution rate >0.15/session without integration intervention
question: Does L-912's integration-bound diagnosis hold when domain frontiers are included in the resolution rate?
method: Classified all frontier closures S382-S412 (31 sessions) into genuine research resolutions vs administrative closures. Measured BOTH global FRONTIER.md and domain-level FRONTIER.md (43 domains). Separated ABANDONED/MERGED (admin) from RESOLVED/CONFIRMED/FALSIFIED (research).

domain_frontier_resolutions.top_domains: stochastic-processes(6), expert-swarm(4), governance(4), quality(4), brain(3), conflict(3), helper-swarm(3), strategy(3)

analysis.missed_pool: Domain frontiers (119 active across 43 domains, 7x larger pool)
analysis.global_only_finding: 0.16/session — barely above 0.15 threshold, L-912 nearly correct for its scope
analysis.combined_finding: 1.71/session — 11.4x above threshold, integration actively happening at domain level
analysis.root_cause: L-912 measured 1 of 2 frontier pools. Domain frontier resolution is 10x global rate. Single-metric integration diagnosis is incomplete.
analysis.admin_inflation: 26/31 global closures were ABANDONED/MERGED (admin triage), not research. Raw counts inflate resolution rate 6x.

verdict_detail: L-912's specific global frontier measurement (0.08/session) is approximately correct (actual: 0.16). But the integration-bound DIAGNOSIS is partially falsified because domain frontiers — the primary resolution mechanism — resolve at 1.55/session. The swarm is NOT integration-bound at the domain level; it IS integration-bound at the global synthesis level. Two regimes coexist.
